findings.justification: Nonce is read from $_REQUEST which includes GET parameters, allowing the nonce to be leaked via Referer headers when it appears in the URL.

Attacker-supplied capability names from $_REQUEST['capabilities'] (only sanitize_key applied) are passed directly into WP_User_Query, enabling unauthenticated (pre-check bypass) or admin-controlled user enumeration by arbitrary capability.
Full WP_User objects (returned by get_results()) are stored in the wp_options table, leaking PII and user metadata to any code that reads 'um_secure_scanned_details'.

$might_affected_users is only assigned inside the if($suspicious_accounts_count > 0) block but is accessed unconditionally at line 263, causing a fatal PHP error when no suspicious accounts are found.

get_option('um_secure_scanned_details', $susp_accounts) falls back to raw user query results when the option is missing, then immediately accesses $option['scanned_caps'] which will be an invalid offset causing a PHP warning and potential undefined-variable confusion.
Active plugin list is retrieved via apply_filters('active_plugins', ...) which any loaded plugin can modify, allowing a malicious plugin to spoof its own presence or absence during the security scan.

scan_recommendations() is a public method with no capability or nonce check; it reads sensitive wp_options data and performs heavy DB queries, and could be invoked from outside the intended AJAX flow.
